name: G2G
description: A global peer-to-peer marketplace where gamers buy and sell in-game items, accounts, boosting and coaching services.

details.targetMarket: Gamers and semi-professional sellers offering in-game currency, items, accounts, boosting and coaching services globally

items: GamerProtect escrow and dispute process (escrows buyer payment until confirmation)
Seller verification (ID eKYC required to be verified)
Insurance period for sold accounts (example: 14 days)
Wide global payment support (many localized methods and currencies)
Commission-based fees for sellers (no listing fee)
Proof-of-delivery requirement for sellers and detailed support center articles

missingFeatures: No standardized/packaged coaching booking flow or recurring-subscription option for repeat coaching clients (coaching is listed but there is no first-class scheduling + subscription product)
No verified-skill or accreditation layer for high-risk services (boosters/coaches) beyond seller ratings and optional ID verification, making quality inconsistent for expensive services
Limited buyer guarantees for long-term assets — account insurance windows are short (e.g., 14 days) which leaves buyers exposed for account bans or later problems
No public API or B2B partner program for third parties to integrate catalogue, verification or payments (hindering platform partnerships and enterprise use cases)
Marketplace discovery and price-comparison is seller-by-seller with weak package standardization, which makes it hard for buyers to compare service quality and create trust for big-ticket purchases
Dispute resolution transparency — users report slow or canned support responses (an opening for a competitor with faster, more transparent claims handling)

pricingPlans.name: Buyer
pricingPlans.note: Buyers pay per-order prices set by individual sellers; platform holds payment in escrow (GamerProtect).

pricingPlans.name: Seller (commission)
pricingPlans.note: No registration/listing fee; commission is charged on successful transactions and varies by product type and seller rank (promo rates frequently applied).

pricingEvidence.label: What Is the Commission Fee to Sell at G2G
pricingEvidence.claim: G2G charges a commission on successful transactions (examples and ranges shown; promotional 4.99% applied to many categories).

pricingEvidence.label: Gift cards FAQ (commission example)
pricingEvidence.claim: Support article states commission typically ranges between 4.99% and 9.99%, with promotional 4.99% for many sellers.

companyEvidence.label: G2G homepage
companyEvidence.claim: G2G is a global digital marketplace for in-game items, accounts, boosting and coaching.

companyEvidence.label: Triple-A partnership announcement
companyEvidence.claim: Article states G2G has over 2.7 million registered users and supports 200+ payment options (used as evidence of scale and payment coverage).

companyEvidence.label: LinkedIn company page
companyEvidence.claim: LinkedIn lists G2G (Gamer2Gamer) with company details and shows office location and employee-size range (used to estimate headcount and HQ).

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Large number of customer reviews (50k+) with repeated complaints about customer support responsiveness and mixed seller reliability.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit community threads
reviewEvidence.claim: Longstanding Reddit threads show user-reported issues with disputes, KYC requests and inconsistent seller behaviour.

featureEvidence.label: GamerProtect (escrow & dispute process)
featureEvidence.claim: GamerProtect holds buyer payments in escrow and defines the platform's dispute resolution and seller protection mechanisms.

featureEvidence.label: Types of services on G2G
featureEvidence.claim: Support article enumerates marketplace categories: game coins, items, accounts, boosting, coaching, gift cards.

featureEvidence.label: Insurance period for accounts
featureEvidence.claim: G2G states that GamerProtect insurance for sold game accounts ends after 14 days.

strengths: Large two-sided marketplace and liquidity — millions of registered users and a very large catalogue across many games, which helps with selection and price competition
Robust global payments coverage (many localized methods) and partnerships (e.g., Triple-A) that lower friction for international buyers/sellers
GamerProtect escrow, seller ranking and proof-of-delivery flow provide a baseline trust model that reduces straightforward scams compared to off-platform trades
No listing/registration fees and simple commission model lowers seller onboarding friction and increases supply-side liquidity
weaknesses: Quality and trust depend heavily on individual sellers — limited standardized vetting for high-risk services (boosting/coaching) which leads to inconsistent service quality
Repeated complaints about slow or formulaic customer support and dispute handling (Trustpilot/Reddit), undermining buyer confidence for higher-value purchases
Short insurance windows for accounts (14 days) and many exclusions in GamerProtect leave buyers exposed to post-delivery problems like bans or account recovery
Discovery and comparison friction — seller-by-seller pricing and non-standardized packages make it hard for buyers to compare offers or buy recurring services easily
No obvious public API or partner/B2B program limits integrations with game studios, esports teams or large sellers who need automation
